# Precept

> A cognitive enforcement layer for AI software development. Sits between your AI agent and your repo over MCP, and forces the agent to understand a change — business logic, blast radius, reusable assets, team decisions — BEFORE it writes code. Returns a binding verdict (proceed/warn/ask/reject), computed rule-based with no LLM. Not another coding agent; it works alongside the one you use (Claude Code, Cursor, Cline).

Knowledge is passive; cognition must be enforced. Markdown docs are optional — agents skip them. Precept makes understanding a required MCP tool call. Team memory is human-approved and git-synced, so every agent in every repo shares one governed brain.

## Quickstart

- Install: `uv tool install --force git+https://github.com/qorstack/preceptai.git`
- Set up: make a dedicated folder (e.g. `~/precept`), `cd` into it, run `precept quickstart`. It writes `.env` + `docker-compose.yml` INTO THAT FOLDER (the Postgres + dashboard stack lives there), registers the MCP server globally with Claude Code, and installs the `/precept` slash commands. Dashboard at http://localhost:9080.
- Update: `precept quickstart --force` (pulls latest image, re-registers MCP, .env preserved).
- Use: in any repo, `/precept add Google SSO to /login` (Claude Code) or `precept "add Google SSO to /login"` (terminal).

## Where things land

- `.env` + `docker-compose.yml` → the folder you ran quickstart in (CWD).
- MCP server + slash commands → global (Claude Code user config + ~/.claude/commands/), so they work in every repo.
- Per-repo: add `precept.config` (workspace + repo_name) at each repo root for workspace-scoped memory.

## Docs

- AGENTS.md — full agent integration guide (setup prompt, mandatory MCP workflow, where files go, team knowledge).
- README.md — human guide: quickstart, manual setup, CI/pre-commit enforcement, dashboard tour.
- `precept mcp-config <client>` — MCP config for Cursor / VS Code / Windsurf / Cline.
- Dashboard (http://localhost:9080) — approve AI memory, browse team knowledge, audit log.

## Notes

- The per-repo config file is named `precept.config` exactly (not `precept-ai.config`); the wrong name makes memory fall back to global scope.
- Ports are non-standard on purpose: dashboard 9080, Postgres 55432.
- Verdicts are advisory until wired into CI/pre-commit: `precept check --strict` (exit 0/1/2).
